retrieved_docs.title: Physiology, Tooth -- Mechanism
retrieved_docs.content: The concept of the envelope of occlusion helps us understand how the teeth should function physiologically. When a patient bites together so that the teeth touch in a normal position (an excellent cue to help patients do this is to ask them to bite on their back teeth) and then slides the lower jaw forward slowly, one can note that the front teeth slide against each other, but the back teeth open and no longer touch. This is called anterior guidance and is an important secondary function of the anterior teeth (the incisors and canines). This function minimizes shear forces on the posterior teeth.

retrieved_docs.title: Physiology, Tooth -- Mechanism
retrieved_docs.content: Starting from normal occlusion with the teeth together, sliding the lower jaw to one side causes the teeth on the same (ipsilateral) side to make contact, while those on the opposite (contralateral) side do not. In some individuals, only the canines make contact during this movement. This pattern helps minimize shear forces during excursive, or sideways, jaw movements. [24] Beyond mastication, teeth have an essential role in phonation. For example, when a fricative sound is made, like saying the letter F, one can note that the maxillary incisors rest against or close to the vermillion border of the lower lip. Finally, teeth, like bones, also participate in mineral exchange. The tooth's inner layers, closest to the circulatory supply and pulp, are most active in this regard, although the enamel may exchange minerals with saliva. [25]

retrieved_docs.title: Physiology, Tooth -- Mechanism
retrieved_docs.content: Occlusion is the term for how teeth fit together and interlock. In proper occlusion, the maxillary arch and teeth sit slightly anterior to the mandibular arch and teeth. In a transverse dimension, the maxillary teeth sit more laterally than the mandibular teeth. Correct occlusion is essential for the primary function of teeth, as a tight meshwork allows for efficient mastication. When this fitment is not ideal, it is called malocclusion, discussed in a later section.

retrieved_docs.title: Physiology, Tooth -- Mechanism
retrieved_docs.content: These mechanics translate to the relationship of the arches to one another. The lower jaw can protrude and move side-to-side (ie, laterally). Together, the range of these movements is called the envelope of occlusion. [22] This envelope allows for idealized tooth mechanics, which minimize shear forces and maximize vertical ones. Teeth sit in the jawbone but do not directly touch the bone. They are embedded in what amounts to a shock absorber called the periodontal ligament. This system is designed to withstand and exert forces in a vertical vector (chewing), which they can do effectively with hundreds of pounds or kilograms of vertical force. [23] The teeth are weakest in shear (lateral) forces. Therefore, all the anatomic and mechanical concepts outlined above work in concert to allow the teeth to grind, mix, and cut food vertically while avoiding shear forces on them.

retrieved_docs.title: Physiology, Tooth -- Mechanism
retrieved_docs.content: To better understand how teeth function, one must understand the mechanics of the temporomandibular joint. The temporomandibular joint is located where the mandible articulates with the skull, directly in front of the auditory canal. Numerous muscles and ligaments guide and control the temporomandibular joint, but at the most basic level, the mandible has 2 possible movements: rotation and translation. When the mouth initially opens, the mandible rotates and then slides forward at a certain point to allow further opening, a process known as translation. This translational movement occurs by 2 joint spaces within the joint and a cartilaginous disc between them, making it a ginglymoarthrodial joint; it both hinges and slides. [21]

retrieved_docs.title: Physiology, Swallowing -- Mechanism -- Pharyngeal Phase
retrieved_docs.content: The bolus is moved inferiorly by a peristaltic-like sequential contraction of the superior, middle, and inferior pharyngeal constrictor muscles in a top to bottom fashion. [9] A pattern of rapid stimulation and inhibition in the pharyngeal muscles creates a cranial-caudal wave of pressure that directs the food bolus towards the UES. [3] The speed is remarkably fast, occurring at rates of 20 to 40cm/s. [10] Once initiated, the pharyngeal phase behaves in an ‘all or none’ fashion, much like a reflex, hence why this phase is considered an irreversible step. 5.) Transit through UES

retrieved_docs.title: Anatomy, Head and Neck, Tooth Eruption -- Structure and Function -- Post-occlusal eruption
retrieved_docs.content: The mechanism underlying horizontal movements remains a subject of investigation. One hypothesis suggests that horizontal displacements, such as buccal movement, occur through a coordinated process involving bone resorption, formation, and cementum apposition. [3] Cementum apposition may also be partly responsible for the compensatory eruption observed in tooth wear. [3] Teeth can continue to undergo eruption even in adulthood as a compensatory response to the loss of an opposing tooth, demonstrating that eruption activity persists throughout an individual's life. [4]

retrieved_docs.title: Anatomy, Head and Neck: Buccinator Muscle -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: The buccinator, orbicularis oris, and pharyngeal constrictors form a functional unit—the buccinator mechanism—essential to orofacial functions like swallowing, sucking, whistling, chewing, and vowel pronunciation. Food accumulates in the vestibule during chewing if the buccinator is weakened or paralyzed.

retrieved_docs.title: Physiology, Swallowing -- Mechanism -- Oral Stage
retrieved_docs.content: For liquids, the bolus is first sealed in the oral cavity by the tongue anteriorly, and hard palate posteriorly, in the preparatory phase. For solids, the bolus is not sealed in the oral cavity as it undergoes processing via mastication and manipulation. 2.) Oral Propulsion The tongue elevates to move the bolus posteriorly into the oropharynx. This bolus is held in the oropharynx until it aggregates with repeated cycles, or the pharyngeal phase initiates. [1]

retrieved_docs.title: Physiology, Tooth -- Function
retrieved_docs.content: Due to its high mineral content, enamel, which covers the visible part of the tooth (the crown), is the hardest substance in the human body, and for a good reason. [7] Teeth must maintain the ability to be fracture-resistant and exert extreme amounts of force and pressure to perform their functions. The primary function of teeth is mastication, which involves the cutting, mixing, and grinding of food to allow the tongue and oropharynx to shape it into a bolus that can be swallowed. [15] The teeth are generally conceptualized as a U-shape, with the bottom of the U representing the front teeth. The upper arch is the maxillary arch, with the teeth embedded in the maxilla, and the lower arch is the mandibular arch, with the teeth embedded in the mandible. The shape of the crown, in combination with arch mechanics and its position in the mouth, determines a tooth’s function in chewing.

retrieved_docs.title: Physiology, Nutrient Absorption -- Function -- Mouth
retrieved_docs.content: The mouth is comprised of the lips, teeth, tongue, salivary glands, hard palate, soft palate, uvula, and oropharynx. The lips are fleshy, movable structures surrounding the mouth's opening. They play a crucial role in speech, expression, and sealing the mouth during eating and drinking. The teeth are hard structures responsible for breaking down food into smaller, more digestible particles. They also aid in oral cavity protection and speech. The tongue is a muscular organ on the oral cavity floor. It is crucial in taste perception, food bolus manipulation, initiating swallowing, and generating the gag reflex. [26]

retrieved_docs.title: Anatomy, Head and Neck, Orbicularis Oris Muscle -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: Orbicularis oris muscle along with the buccinator and pharyngeal constrictor form a functional unit, known as "buccinator mechanism" which has an important role in orofacial function (swallowing, sucking, whistling, chewing, vowel pronunciation, kissing). [17] If buccinator and orbicularis oris muscles are weakened or paralyzed, food tends to accumulate in the oral vestibule during chewing.

retrieved_docs.title: Physiology, Swallowing -- Mechanism -- Pharyngeal Phase
retrieved_docs.content: The processes mentioned above are mostly under voluntary muscle control. The pharyngeal phase, the first irreversible step in the swallowing mechanism, begins when the bolus reaches the palatoglossal arch. Afferent sensory fibers from CN IX, X, and XI in the oropharynx transmit the stimulus to the solitary tract nucleus in the brainstem. [3] Efferent muscle fibers then travel to innervate the muscles of the larynx, pharynx, and esophagus to coordinate a reflex response. This stage serves two primary purposes: 1) directs food into the esophagus, and 2) protects the airway from aspiration. It is characterized by a wave of coordinated stimuli lasting about a second that ends as the food bolus reaches the upper esophageal sphincter (UES). [4] 1.) Nasopharynx closure The pharyngeal phase beings with soft palate elevation via the tensor palatini and levator palatini to seal the nasopharynx to prevent pressure escape into the nasal cavity. 2.) Airway protection

retrieved_docs.title: Anatomy, Head and Neck, Teeth -- Structure and Function -- Function
retrieved_docs.content: The basic function of teeth mastication of food to create a swallowable food bolus. Teeth also play a crucial role in speech and facial aesthetics. Teeth provide a masticatory system that functions in the incising, tearing and grinding of food. The World Health Organization (WHO) considers the number of teeth to be a key indicator of oral health status. Retention of function, aesthetics, and natural dentition of at least 20 teeth with nine to ten occluding pairs (including anterior teeth) is associated with adequate masticatory efficiency and ability. Teeth also play a role in satisfaction and facial aesthetics. Loss of anterior teeth has shown to impair aesthetics and satisfaction for most people. Although complete dentition is not necessary to satisfy oral function needs, a low functional level may lead to malnutrition and compromised physiological and psychological health. [1] [2] [6]

retrieved_docs.title: Anatomy, Head and Neck, Palatoglossus Muscle (Glossopalatinus, Palatoglossal) -- Physiologic Variants
retrieved_docs.content: Variation in the region of attachment of the palatoglossus muscle in the soft palate has been observed. In some individuals, this region is near the uvula. In such cases, the mechanical ability of the palatoglossus muscle in elevating the tongue may be limited due to the lack of a rigid anchoring point towards which the tongue elevates. However, this attachment is favorable to lower the soft palate. This action is based on a class II lever system where the load (the bulk of soft palate) is present between the fulcrum (the posterior rim of the hard palate) and the force applied (action of the palatoglossus muscle). In some individuals, the attachment area of the muscle is relatively large into the anterior portion of the velum. In such instances, a considerable increase in the ability of the muscle to elevate the tongue exists. However, this attachment is not favorable to lower the soft palate. This action is based on a class III lever system where the force applied lies between the load and the fulcrum. The primary mechanism in these individuals to approximate the tongue and soft palate during swallowing is tongue elevation rather than lowering of the soft palate. [1]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 3 -- Community 2187
retrieved_docs.content: Posterior repair of vagina is a surgical intervention that directly involves the vagina, aiming to restore its structural integrity. Vesicovaginal fistula with involvement of urinary continence mechanism following normal delivery is a pathological condition that is located in the vagina, often compromising urinary continence after childbirth. Both concepts underscore the clinical importance of the vagina as a site for therapeutic repair and as a potential location of obstetric complications affecting urinary function.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 5 -- Level 3 -- Community 2539
retrieved_docs.content: Vesicovaginal fistula with involvement of urinary continence mechanism following normal delivery is a condition that is found in the vagina.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 28 -- Level 1 -- Community 186
retrieved_docs.content: with fistula formation when treated; treatment of bladder diverticulum (علاج القيلة المثانية) and treatment of rectal diverticulum (علاج قيلة مستقيمية) are both noted to have potential fistulous complications. All of these conditions share the common pathophysiological mechanism of organ descent or herniation into the vaginal canal, and their management frequently involves direct intervention on the vaginal walls, correction of obstructive changes, and prevention or treatment of fistulous sequelae.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 27 -- Level 3 -- Community 2530
retrieved_docs.content: Plastic repair of urethrocele in female is a surgical intervention that is performed directly on the vagina, and its direct morphology is described as “انسدال,” indicating an obstructive or closure component. Posterior repair of vagina is another procedure that is specifically related to the vagina, emphasizing the organ’s central role in pelvic reconstructive surgery. Vesicovaginal fistula with involvement of the urinary continence mechanism following normal delivery is a pathological condition whose finding site is the vagina, highlighting that defects of the urinary continence system after childbirth are localized to this same anatomical region. Together, these concepts illustrate how both therapeutic procedures (plastic repair of urethrocele and posterior vaginal repair) and disease processes (vesicovaginal fistula affecting continence) are interconnected through their focus on the vagina, underscoring the importance of detailed anatomical and morphological understanding—such as recognizing obstruction (“انسدال”)—for effective patient assessment and management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 5 -- Level 2 -- Community 1186
retrieved_docs.content: Formation of urethrovaginal fistula is identified as a urethrovaginaal fistel condition that directly involves the vagina and is classified under the broader category of هيكلة المهبل (vaginal structuring). Vesicovaginal fistula with involvement of the urinary continence mechanism following normal delivery also has its primary finding site in the vagina, underscoring the vagina as a common anatomical location for fistulous pathology. Reconstruction of vagina with urethral dissection is a surgical intervention that is performed directly on the vagina and is regarded as a type of جراحة المهبل التجميلية (cosmetic vaginal surgery). Together, these concepts illustrate the interrelationship between vaginal fistula formation, the anatomical focus of the vagina in both disease and repair, and the role of reconstructive and cosmetic surgical techniques in managing such conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 2 -- Community 800
retrieved_docs.content: The classification “other pregnancy and delivery including normal” functions as a parent concept that encompasses a broad range of normal obstetric conditions and their outcomes, as reflected by its membership in the SMQ “حالات الحمل الطبيعي ونتائجه (SMQ).” Within this SMQ, specific entities such as “توسيع عنق الرحم” (cervical dilation) and “الكَلَف” (post‑partum hemorrhage) are listed as members, illustrating concrete clinical scenarios that arise in uncomplicated pregnancies. A more detailed subcategory, “NORMAL DELIVERY, AND OTHER INDICATIONS FOR CARE IN PREGNANCY, LABOR, AND DELIVERY,” is a child of the parent concept and includes conditions that may require additional monitoring or intervention during a normal delivery. One of its children is “Malposition and malpresentation of fetus,” highlighting fetal positioning problems that can occur even in otherwise routine births. The entity “Vesicovaginal fistula with involvement of urinary continence mechanism following normal delivery”

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 3 -- Community 2047
retrieved_docs.content: The category “other pregnancy and delivery including normal” serves as a broad classification for conditions and care considerations related to normal childbirth. Within this overarching group, the subcategory “NORMAL DELIVERY, AND OTHER INDICATIONS FOR CARE IN PREGNANCY, LABOR, AND DELIVERY” is specifically designated for care issues associated with normal delivery, and it includes the condition “Malposition and malpresentation of fetus” as a child topic, highlighting that fetal positioning problems are addressed under normal delivery care protocols. Additionally, “Vesicovaginal fistula with involvement of urinary continence mechanism following normal delivery” is identified as a distinct type of complication that also falls under the broader “other pregnancy and delivery including normal” classification, emphasizing its relevance as a specific adverse outcome linked to normal vaginal delivery. This hierarchical organization clarifies how various fetal presentation abnormalities and postpartum complications are related to and categorized within the context of normal pregnancy and delivery care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 27 -- Level 2 -- Community 1197
retrieved_docs.content: fistula is identified as a type of vaginal structuring (هيكلة المهبل) and is directly related to the vagina as its procedural site. The condition itself is described as a urethrovaginal fistel, emphasizing the presence of a fistulous connection between the urethra and vagina. Plastic repair of urethrocele in females is performed through a vaginal approach, targeting an obstructive (انسدال) morphology of the urethral support structures. Similarly, reconstruction of the vagina with urethral dissection is carried out via the vagina and is classified under cosmetic vaginal surgery (جراحة المهبل التجميلية), highlighting its role in both functional restoration and aesthetic improvement. Vesicovaginal fistula that involves the urinary continence mechanism after normal delivery is identified as a finding located in the vagina, underscoring the importance of vaginal assessment in postpartum fistula evaluation. Additionally, a plastic operation on the urethral sphincter performed by a vaginal

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 28 -- Level 2 -- Community 587
retrieved_docs.content: Vesicovaginal fistula with involvement of urinary continence mechanism following normal delivery is classified as a complication within the broader category of other pregnancy and delivery including normal events. This specific fistula occurs at the site of the vagina, reflecting a disruption of the urinary continence mechanism that can arise after an uncomplicated vaginal birth. Posterior repair of vagina, a surgical procedure that directly addresses pathology of the vaginal wall, is closely related to the vagina and may be employed as a therapeutic option to reconstruct and restore normal anatomy and function in cases of vesicovaginal fistula involving the continence mechanism. Understanding the relationship between this fistula, its anatomical location, and the appropriate posterior vaginal repair technique is essential for effective patient management following normal delivery complications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 2287
retrieved_docs.content: MATERNAL AND FETAL CONDITIONS AFFECTING LABOR AND DELIVERY is a broad category that includes both normal physiological processes and specific obstetric conditions that influence the course of labor and the outcome of delivery. Within this category, normal pregnancy and/or delivery—particularly spontaneous vaginal delivery—and normal delivery procedures are recognized as standard pathways that support uncomplicated birth. The category also encompasses a range of pelvic contraction events, including تقلص الحوض (pelvic contraction), تقلص مخرج الحوض (contraction of the pelvic outlet), and تقلص مدخل الحوض (contraction of the pelvic inlet), which are important physiological mechanisms that facilitate fetal descent and expulsion during labor. Additionally, it addresses fetal positioning issues such as سوء تموضع الجنين (fetal malposition) and breech malpresentation successfully converted to cephalic presentation, highlighting that a breech presentation can be managed by converting the fetus to a head‑first (cephalic) position to improve delivery outcomes. Together, these elements illustrate how normal labor processes, pelvic biomechanics, and the management of fetal malpositions are interrelated components of maternal and fetal conditions that affect labor and delivery, guiding clinicians in optimizing care and ensuring safe, effective childbirth.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 1 -- Community 179
retrieved_docs.content: in female (performed on the vagina and characterized by انسدال), Reconstruction of vagina with urethral dissection (classified as جراحة المهبل التجميلية – cosmetic vaginal surgery), Posterior repair of vagina, and Repair of vaginal wall prolapse, all aimed at restoring vaginal wall integrity, which is the anatomical location of تدلّي المهبل. Vesicovaginal fistula with involvement of the urinary continence mechanism after normal delivery is another pathology found in the vagina, underscoring the importance of vaginal integrity for continence. Treatment approaches for bladder and rectal prolapse (علاج القيلة المثانية and علاج قيلة مستقيمية) are both associated with فَتْك, indicating that fistula formation is a common therapeutic concern in managing these prolapses. Collectively, these entities and their interrelationships illustrate the complex anatomy, classification, pathology, and surgical management of female pelvic organ prolapse and related urinary‑genital disorders.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 22 -- Level 3 -- Community 2184
retrieved_docs.content: Formation of urethrovaginal fistula, which is classified as a type of هيكلة المهبل (vaginal structuring), is directly related to the vagina and is linked to the entity urethrovaginaal; fistel. Plastic repair of urethrocele in female also has the vagina as its direct procedural site and is characterized by the morphology انسدال. Posterior repair of vagina is a procedure that has a direct relationship with the vagina. Reconstruction of vagina with urethral dissection is performed on the vagina and is identified as a form of جراحة المهبل التجميلية (cosmetic vaginal surgery). Vesicovaginal fistula with involvement of the urinary continence mechanism following normal delivery is found at the vagina. Together, these conditions and surgical interventions underscore the variety of fistulous disorders and reconstructive or reparative procedures that focus on vaginal anatomy, emphasizing the need for site‑specific management in female pelvic health.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 12 -- Level 2 -- Community 788
retrieved_docs.content: Posterior repair of vagina is a surgical procedure that directly involves the vagina, and Reconstruction of vagina with urethral dissection is another operative technique performed on the vagina. The latter procedure is classified as جراحة المهبل التجميلية, indicating that it is considered a form of cosmetic vaginal surgery. Vesicovaginal fistula with involvement of urinary continence mechanism following normal delivery is a pathological condition that is identified in the vagina. Together, these concepts illustrate that both reparative and reconstructive surgeries, as well as certain obstetric complications, are centered on the vaginal anatomy, and that some reconstructive interventions are specifically categorized as cosmetic procedures, which is important for clinical decision‑making and patient counseling.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 9 -- Level 2 -- Community 935
retrieved_docs.content: The concept “NORMAL DELIVERY, AND OTHER INDICATIONS FOR CARE IN PREGNANCY, LABOR, AND DELIVERY” is situated within the broader category “other pregnancy and delivery including normal,” indicating that it represents a specific subset of pregnancy‑related care that includes routine vaginal birth as well as additional clinical indications. Within this framework, the condition “Vesicovaginal fistula with involvement of urinary continence mechanism following normal delivery” is classified as a type of “other pregnancy and delivery including normal,” highlighting that this fistula can arise as a complication after an otherwise normal birth. The category “other pregnancy and delivery including normal” is described as having a narrower relationship to the Arabic term “مخاض غير طبيعي” (abnormal labor), suggesting that while it focuses on normal and related deliveries, it is encompassed by the larger spectrum of labor types that also includes abnormal presentations. Tubal surgical interventions intersect extensively with intrauterine pregnancy. Procedures such as ligation of bilateral fallopian tubes, ligation and crushing of bilateral fallopian tubes, and the broader category ligation or occlusion of fallopian tubes (ربط البوق) are clinically associated with both intrauterine pregnancy and cesarean section. “Ligation and crushing of bilateral fallopian tubes” is a specific form of both “ligation of bilateral fallopian tubes” and the general “ligation or occlusion of fallopian tubes,” illustrating a hierarchical relationship among these techniques. The entity “Vesicovaginal fistula with involvement of urinary continence mechanism following normal delivery” is classified as an instance of “other pregnancy and delivery including normal,” indicating that this specific fistula can develop as a complication of a normal delivery. The system also links normal delivery concepts to abnormal labor through a “has a narrower relationship” connection between “other pregnancy and delivery including normal” and the Arabic term “مخاض غير طبيعي” (abnormal labor). This relationship underscores that while the primary focus is on normal obstetric processes, there is a defined pathway to more complex or pathological labor scenarios.
